labels.name: get_information_yield
labels.description: Information Yield (IY) — how much a confirmed call should move a skeptic's belief, in BITS of surprise-if-true (log2 of the published 1-in-N prior, capped at 1-in-a-million; earned = surprise × verdict-credit). A base rate / consensus-follower scores ZERO bits by construction — the metric on which t

labels.name: get_warning_timeline
labels.description: The 'before-the-event' indications-and-warning / after-action timeline for a named event, by advisory id (e.g. 'LA-022') or slug (e.g. 'new-glenn-ng3', 'crocus'). labels.name: get_regrade_kit
labels.description: The grade-it-yourself kit: inputs to recompute the record's Brier (calibration), named-mechanism specificity, AND Information Yield under YOUR OWN verdicts — plus the one-step stress-test recipes (harsh-verdicts, externally-adjudicated-only, estimative-worst-case, …). labels.name: get_luck_test
labels.description: The corpus-level 'could this record be luck?' significance test, computed AGAINST the record: 92 graded calls clustered into 68 independent events (correlated calls share one event), strict scoring (one NEAR fails the whole event), luck-prior floored at a coin flip per event.
